title.en: Red onion — dry zone Maha cultivation calendar
title.si: රතු ලූනු වගා කාල සටහන — වියළි කලාපය

summary.en: A 48-week plan covering commercial red-onion cultivation in Sri Lanka's dry zone during the Maha season (Dec–May), followed by a seed-bulb production cycle (Jun–Nov). Inputs, pest calendar, and economics are from the DOA / AFACI 2014 bulletin; districts in scope: Anuradhapura, Puttalam, Kurunegala, Polonnaruwa, Matale, Monaragala, Vavuniya, Mannar, Mullaitivu, Kilinochchi, Trincomalee, Hambantota, Ampara, Badulla (low-country portions).
summary.si: වියළි කලාපයේ මහ කන්නයේදී (දෙසැම්බර්–මැයි) රතු ලූනු වාණිජ වගාවෙන් ආරම්භ වී, යාල කන්නයේදී (ජූනි–නොවැම්බර්) බීජ බල්බ නිෂ්පාදනය දක්වා දිවෙන සති 48ක සම්පූර්ණ කාල සටහන.

source_document_title: DOA / AFACI — Red Onion Cultivation Calendar, Dry Zone (2014)

field_provenance.source: hand-transcribed from DOA / AFACI infographic provided by the maintainer
field_provenance.notes: Sinhala text on the source is the canonical authoring locale; English summary added by the ingestion agent. Tamil translation pending agronomist review.

body.en: Deep ploughing and secondary tillage. Prepare raised beds 90 × 800 cm with 60 cm paths between beds; finer beds 60 × 800 cm where transplanting spacing is 10 × 10 cm.
Incorporate well-decomposed compost 10–15 t/ha at least 1–2 weeks before sowing.
Apply basal dose 1–2 weeks before sowing: Urea 27, TSP 40, MOP 20 kg/acre. Ammonium sulphate 40 kg/acre as supplemental sulphur on sandy soils.
Sow direct seed or transplant 25–30-day seedlings. Row × plant spacing 15 × 15 cm for commercial beds; 10 × 10 cm for seed-production beds.
Apply pre-emergence herbicide (oxyfluorfen 23.5 EC 500 ml/ha) within 3 days of sowing, before weed emergence.
Hand-weed between rows; avoid disturbing shallow bulb roots.
Weekly irrigation to field capacity; avoid waterlogging. Reduce 10–14 days before harvest to promote bulb curing.

title.en: Top-dressing — week 10 (30 days in Sinhala convention)

body.en: Second top-dressing approximately 10 weeks after sowing.
Harvest when 75–80% of tops have fallen over. Cure in shade 7–10 days before grading. Expected yield: 3,000–3,800 kg/acre.
Select medium-sized (20–30 g) disease-free bulbs; cure and hold 8–10 weeks for vernalization before the seed-production planting.

notes.en: Purple blotch (Alternaria porri). Small purple lesions with concentric rings on older leaves — favoured by high humidity and dew. Spray at first lesions.
Anthracnose (Colletotrichum). Twisting of leaves with orange fruiting bodies; worse with overhead irrigation. Treatment per DOA bulletin: Captan 50% WP, 6 g per 10 L water.
Downy mildew (Peronospora destructor). Pale-yellow oval lesions with violet sporulation in dew. Treatment: Metalaxyl+Mancozeb 72 WP 20 g/10 L; second spray 10 days later if damp weather continues.
Fungal bulb rot complex (Fusarium / Sclerotium). Drench neck and basal plate: Mancozeb 75 WP 15 g/10 L at first symptom. Avoid mechanical injury at weeding.

Bacterial bulb rot (Burkholderia cepacia). Soft watery rot with foul smell, typically after heavy rain. Treatment per DOA: Copper oxychloride 50% WP, 5 g / 4 g per 10 L water. Cull and destroy infected plants.
Onion stem fly (Delia antiqua) — Sinhala: පැලමැත්කා. Wilting central leaves and tunnelling in the neck. DOA spray: Profenofos 50 EC 10 ml per 10 L water, two applications 7 days apart.
Onion armyworm (Spodoptera litura / S. exigua) — Sinhala: ලූනු දළඹුවා. Defoliation and bulb scarring. DOA spray: Emamectin benzoate 5 SG 4 g per 10 L water; scout at dusk.

field_provenance.source_note: DOA/AFACI 2014 bulletin, 2013 reference-year estimates. Figures are LKR per acre; adjust for current year before using in planning tools.
